name: Woodminton
overview: Woodminton is a small hamlet on the south-western edge of Bowerchalke parish in the Chalke Valley, south-west Wiltshire, about 11-12 miles from Salisbury and within the Cranborne Chase National Landscape close to the Dorset and Hampshire borders. Recorded as a group of houses and cottages since the 16th century, it strings along a lane off the Broad Chalke-Sixpenny Handley road, with surviving thatched cottages and 19th/20th-century houses. It gives its name to Woodminton Down, part of the Bowerchalke Downs SSSI chalk grassland.
character: A tiny, quiet hamlet of period cottages and farmhouses scattered along a country lane beneath species-rich chalk downland, deep in protected AONB countryside.
amenities: No shops or pub in the hamlet itself
Bowerchalke village has a monthly village-hall market and the Chalke Valley Cricket Club
Broad Chalke (~3 miles) has a pub, community shop/cafe, GP surgery and primary school
Sixpenny Handley (~4 miles) has a primary school, nursery and a well-known butcher

commutes.notes: About 11-12 miles; Salisbury Reds bus serves the valley
From Salisbury, or from Tisbury station (~6 miles) on the Exeter-Waterloo line

prices.summary: A very small hamlet of period and farm houses (e.g. the brick-and-flint Woodminton Farmhouse, c.1860) within a Conservation Area, so sales are rare and values sit high. Use the SP5 sold-price record for context.

thingsToDo: Walking the Bowerchalke Downs SSSI (Woodminton, Marleycombe and Knowle Downs)
Chalk-stream walks along the River Chalke and Garston Wood RSPB reserve nearby
The Chalke Valley History Festival each June
placesToEat: The Queens Head, Broad Chalke (~3 miles)
pros: Beautiful, secluded Chalke Valley and Cranborne Chase setting
Species-rich chalk downland and excellent walking on the doorstep
Village amenities at Bowerchalke, Broad Chalke and Sixpenny Handley nearby
cons: No amenities within the hamlet itself
Car-dependent and around 11-12 miles from Salisbury
whoItSuits: Buyers seeking a tranquil downland hamlet in protected countryside, content to drive a few miles for village amenities.

sources.label: VCH Wiltshire vol. 13: Bower Chalke (Woodminton history)
Bowerchalke area guide - Whites Estate Agents
